# /spec

> Draft the feature spec for a d365-ce feature. Spec is always feature-scoped (per [ADR-0006](../../../design/adr/0006-doc-scope-domain-vs-feature.md)) even though FDD/TDD/blueprint are domain-scoped.

## Usage

```
/spec --feature <slug> [--source fresh|alm] [--project <name>]
```

- `--source fresh` (default) draws from an attached requirement / RFP / user story.
- `--source alm` pulls a work-item set from ADO/JIRA via the `alm` agent (Options 1/2/3 from [design/agents/alm.md](../../../design/agents/alm.md)). The pulled work-item IDs are recorded in `spec.md` frontmatter as `alm-level-locks: [...]`.

## Inputs

- `agents.yaml` — agent registration and docScope
- `project.config.yaml` — publisher prefix, multilingual flags, oobOverrides, unitTestPolicy
- `templates/spec.template.md` — section shape
- Source material (RFP, user story, ALM work items)
- (Optional) Existing `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/` to detect cross-feature impact (§9 of the template)

## Execution flow

1. Validate `--feature` slug against `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$`.
2. Resolve project from `--project` or current working directory.
3. If `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/` does not exist, ask user to run `New-Feature.ps1` first (scaffold scripts own folder creation).
4. Read `project.config.yaml` to absorb publisher prefix + multilingual scope + OOB overrides.
5. (When `--source alm`) Call `alm_pull` MCP tool with the level-lock list; receive raw work-item content; convert via the md-ALM converter; pre-fill §1, §3, §10 of the spec.
6. Render [templates/spec.template.md](../../templates/spec.template.md) into `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/spec.md`.
7. Update `.workflow.json`: `phase=DEFINE`, `currentStates=[SPEC_DRAFT]`, gate `SPEC_APPROVED.status=PENDING`.
8. Log to `.workflow.json.history`.

## Output

- `projects/{p}/d365-ce/features/{f}/spec.md`
- `.workflow.json` updated

## Doc-lint contract

- `frontmatter-required`: yes (feature-id, agent, phase=DEFINE, schema-version, source, gates-passed)
- `ai-summary-required`: yes (`## AI Summary` first non-frontmatter section)
- `traceability-matrix`: yes (§10)
- `no-inline-alm-ids`: yes (ALM ids only in frontmatter `alm-level-locks`)
